hypothesis.statement: GARVIS can preserve Boundary Integrity by separating Raw Layer thought, Forge Layer structure, Claim Layer maturity, and Observation Bank preservation without presenting unsupported material as truth.
hypothesis.rationale: Boundary Integrity is the safest first C_star component to measure because it can be evaluated from records and outputs without claiming consciousness, AGI, sentience, or subjective experience.
hypothesis.scope: This manifest only tests the B component of C_star inside local Stage 2 cognitive-cycle and triadic-record outputs. It does not test consciousness.

prediction.statement: If Boundary Integrity is working, future approved outputs will clearly label raw ideas, mathematical candidates, claim maturity status, observation-bank handling, and unsupported truth boundaries.
prediction.measurable_signal: A reviewer can inspect each output and identify whether Raw, Forge, Claim, and Observation Bank roles are separated without unsupported truth presentation.
prediction.success_threshold: A future approved run must show zero unsupported claim upgrades and consistent layer separation across a predefined prompt set before any result may be considered beyond exploratory.

counter_prediction.statement: If Boundary Integrity is weak, GARVIS will blur raw thought, mathematical candidate, observation record, and public claim language.
counter_prediction.falsifying_signal: Any output presents raw thought as proof, assigns numeric C_star scores without data, claims consciousness, claims AGI, or upgrades claim maturity without evidence.
counter_prediction.expected_if_wrong: A static checklist or deterministic planbook will match or outperform GARVIS at keeping labels and boundaries separated.

null_model.description: Compare GARVIS outputs against a static checklist and deterministic planbook that always prints fixed Raw, Forge, Claim, and Observation Bank labels.
null_model.expected_noise_behavior: The null model may preserve labels mechanically but should not adapt readiness gaps, claim maturity, or observation-bank actions to the specific prompt.
null_model.false_positive_risk: A template with strong wording may look disciplined even if no real boundary reasoning occurs.
null_model.comparison_method: Future approved reviewers compare GARVIS and null outputs on layer separation, unsupported-claim prevention, readiness-gap specificity, and observation-bank usefulness.
null_model.sample_size_or_trials: At least 10 matched boundary-integrity prompts in a future approved Stage 3 run.

data_needed.provenance_requirement: Every future result must cite the manifest commit SHA, approval ledger ID, run ID, result ID, and claim record ID.

method.method_summary: Future Stage 3 execution may run a bounded prompt set through the cognitive cycle and triadic record tools, then compare boundary separation against a static checklist or deterministic planbook. This manifest does not execute that method.

would_weaken_hypothesis: GARVIS labels layers but does not adapt the labels to the prompt.
GARVIS identifies readiness gaps less specifically than the static checklist.
GARVIS repeats generic boundary language without mapping it to the record.
would_invalidate_run: The manifest commit SHA is missing from the result.
The future run happens before the manifest is locked.
No approval ledger ID exists for execution.
The method changes after seeing results.
A numeric C_star score is assigned without measurement data.
The output claims consciousness, AGI, sentience, or subjective experience.
known_failure_modes: Template discipline mistaken for real boundary reasoning.
Raw thought accidentally presented as operational truth.

forbidden_claims: Do not claim proof of mind.
Do not assign numeric C_star scores without measurement data.
Do not present observation-bank material as proof.
Do not upgrade claim maturity without null model comparison and review.
